title: chaenactis glabriuscula var. glabriuscula
intro: Chaenactis glabriuscula var. glabriuscula, also known as the desert cotton or broom weed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the aster family and is native to western North America. The medicinal properties of Chaenactis glabriuscula var. glabriuscula are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from congestion. The herbal preparations of Chaenactis glabriuscula var. glabriuscula can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to the affected area.
